title: Define Meshtastic-to-LICHEN compatibility contract and mapping table
description: Why this exists: firmware code needs a narrow contract that maps Meshtastic app semantics onto LICHEN local resources without implying Meshtastic RF interoperability. What needs to be done: define the adapter boundary, transport framing, protobuf message ownership, request/response mapping, error policy, and unsupported feature behavior for the MVP. Map Meshtastic node info/status/config/message/key operations to LICHEN HAL identity, local CoAP/status/config/message/key flows, or explicit unsupported errors. Acceptance criteria: mapping table is recorded in Beads notes or design field; RF protocol boundary is explicit; config/status/message flows have deterministic behavior; edge cases such as unknown protobufs, missing channels, unsupported admin operations, and app-provided position are specified.
design:
Meshtastic-to-LICHEN compatibility contract for firmware MVP, 2026-06-30.

Scope and boundary:
- This is a local app compatibility adapter for unmodified Meshtastic mobile/Python clients. It is not Meshtastic RF interoperability. LICHEN RF remains IPv6/SCHC/RPL/CoAP over the native LICHEN link, with LICHEN sync word/framing/security.
- Firmware target is Zephyr C, optional and off by default. Rust/Python Meshtastic code remains reference/tooling unless a later Bead selects a different path.
- BLE GATT Meshtastic transport carries one raw protobuf value per characteristic write/read. BLE must reject serial/TCP StreamAPI framing (`0x94 0xc3 <len>`) and must not use NUS UUIDs for Meshtastic compatibility.
- The adapter sits above the shared LICHEN app-level behavioral contract. Direct BLE app protocol and IP/CoAP local-link clients must observe the same node identity/status/config/message semantics.

Transport contract:
- Advertise Meshtastic service UUID `6ba1b218-15a8-461f-9fa8-5dcae273eafd`.
- Expose ToRadio write UUID `f75c76d2-129e-4dad-a1dd-7866124401e7`, FromRadio read UUID `2c55e69e-4993-11ed-b878-0242ac120002`, and FromNum read/notify UUID `ed9da18c-a800-4f66-a670-aa7547e34453`.
- ToRadio payload max is 504 bytes; FromRadio payload max is 510 bytes. Oversized writes fail before decode and must not mutate adapter state. Oversized outbound records must be reduced, split semantically, or replaced by deterministic error/status output; never emit partial protobufs.
- FromNum is only a queue hint. Every queued FromRadio value increments the 32-bit counter modulo 2^32 and notifies subscribed clients. FromRadio read returns exactly one queued protobuf value; zero-length read means queue drained.

Inbound ToRadio mapping:
- `heartbeat`: tolerate at any time. It is a liveness/keepalive input and may queue queueStatus; it is never required before sync.
- `want_config_id = 69420`: stage-1 app sync. Queue synthetic/read-only config, moduleConfig defaults, channel 0, metadata, region_presets policy output, and matching `config_complete_id = 69420`.
- `want_config_id = 69421`: node DB sync. Queue local node_info plus known peer node_info records from the LICHEN peer table/cache, then matching `config_complete_id = 69421`.
- Other `want_config_id`: legacy/full sync. Queue the safe union of stage-1 config/static records and stage-2 node DB records, then echo the client nonce in `config_complete_id`.
- `disconnect`: close or mark the client session as politely ending when transport support exists; otherwise accept as no-op and do not disturb RF/local mesh state.
- `packet` with decoded `TEXT_MESSAGE_APP`: validate UTF-8/payload bounds, map Meshtastic destination to LICHEN destination, submit to the shared LICHEN message contract, queue local queueStatus with the Meshtastic packet id.
- `packet` with decoded `POSITION_APP`: if a LICHEN location provider/app contract is available, map to LICHEN position announce/update; otherwise deterministic unsupported status/notification.
- `packet` with admin/config/channel writes: read-only compatibility data is allowed; writes are deterministic no-op/error and must not change LICHEN RF security, channels, keys, or HAL settings.
- Unknown ToRadio fields or unsupported packet portnums: parse/skip safely when protobuf-valid, then return deterministic unsupported behavior through queueStatus and/or clientNotification where visible feedback is useful. Malformed protobuf rejects the write.

Outbound FromRadio mapping:
- `my_info`: synthetic local node number derived from LICHEN IID low 32 bits, reboot/session counters when available, and harmless firmware/build fields per metadata policy.
- `metadata`: synthetic firmware/device metadata defined by `project-LICHEN-t2hn.15`; must not claim Meshtastic RF firmware behavior.
- `node_info`: local and peer node summaries derived from LICHEN identity, peer table, presence, and optional position/battery providers. Meshtastic node num is low 32 bits of LICHEN IID; collision handling is display-only and must not alter LICHEN routing.
- `config`: read-only synthetic config sufficient for app screens. Radio/config values reflect current LICHEN/HAL facts when known, or safe defaults. Writes do not change LICHEN settings.
- `moduleConfig`: safe defaults only for MVP unless an actual LICHEN feature backs a module.
- `channel`: expose one synthetic primary channel named `LICHEN`; no secondary channels or Meshtastic PSK semantics. Channel writes are no-op/error.
- `region_presets`: emit known-safe presets or a documented absence policy compatible with current apps.
- `config_complete_id`: always echo the nonce for the sync stage just completed.
- `queueStatus`: local adapter enqueue/accounting status only. It is not a delivery receipt.
- `packet` with `TEXT_MESSAGE_APP`: surface incoming LICHEN messages as Meshtastic text packets with stable packet ids, source/destination projections, channel 0, and payload bytes.
- `packet` with `ROUTING_APP`: app-visible delivery success/failure. Correlate with original Meshtastic packet id through `decoded.request_id`; success maps to routing error NONE, failure maps to deterministic routing error where possible.
- `clientNotification`: optional advisory for unsupported operations or compatibility limitations; field number is 16 in the pinned schema.

Identity and address policy:
- Meshtastic node number is the low 32 bits of the LICHEN IID. Meshtastic id string is `!%08x` from that number.
- Broadcast `0xffffffff` maps to the LICHEN app contract's broadcast/all-nodes behavior. Direct destinations require a peer-table reverse lookup from 32-bit node number to full LICHEN identity; no match yields deterministic failure/NAK rather than blind RF send.
- Collision between two peers sharing low 32 bits is a Meshtastic-display ambiguity only; LICHEN must continue using full identities internally. If collision is detected, prefer local deterministic tie-break for display and create a follow-up Bead if it affects app behavior.

Unsupported/deferred MVP behavior:
- No Meshtastic RF packet forwarding or RF compatibility.
- No Meshtastic channel/PSK/key management; LICHEN keys and OSCORE/link security stay authoritative.
- No xmodem/file transfer, MQTT client proxy, store-and-forward, range test, traceroute, audio, TAK/ATAK, lockdown controls, or full admin writes in MVP.
- Unsupported operations must be deterministic: malformed input rejects at transport/codec; valid but unsupported input queues queueStatus failure and/or clientNotification; never silently pretend a mesh-side operation happened.

Implementation dependencies unlocked by this contract:
- `project-LICHEN-t2hn.4`: BLE GATT surface.
- `project-LICHEN-t2hn.5`: raw-protobuf dispatcher and FromRadio queue.
- `project-LICHEN-t2hn.6`: node info/status/config read handlers.
- `project-LICHEN-t2hn.7`: text ingress to LICHEN local interface.
- `project-LICHEN-t2hn.8`: incoming LICHEN messages/status to Meshtastic app.
- `project-LICHEN-t2hn.9`: explicit unsupported-operation errors.
notes: 2026-06-30 completed design contract in Beads design field. Contract uses primary-source research from project-LICHEN-t2hn.1, architecture decision from project-LICHEN-t2hn.17, and bounded Zephyr codec behavior from closed project-LICHEN-t2hn.3. Acceptance covered: explicit RF boundary, BLE raw-protobuf transport, protobuf ownership, deterministic sync/config/status/message flows, unsupported operations, unknown/malformed protobuf policy, missing channels, admin writes, app position, address mapping, FromNum queue semantics, queueStatus vs ROUTING_APP delivery receipts. No source code changed for this Bead, so the three-code-review rule did not apply.
close_reason: Completed: Meshtastic-to-LICHEN MVP adapter contract and mapping table recorded in Beads design field.
